annotations.label: Homeomorphism

anchor.snippet: is a bijective and continuous function between topological spaces that has a continuous inverse function

annotations.note: `Homeomorph X Y` bundles an equivalence with continuous forward and inverse maps, matching the lead description.

annotations.label: Homeomorphic spaces

anchor.snippet: Two spaces with a homeomorphism between them are called homeomorphic

annotations.note: Mathlib provides the type `X ≃ₜ Y` (so `Nonempty (X ≃ₜ Y)` expresses 'homeomorphic'), but has no named predicate like `IsHomeomorphicTo`.

annotations.label: Homeomorphism (formal)

anchor.snippet: is a homeomorphism if it has the following properties

annotations.note: The unbundled `IsHomeomorph f` predicate (continuous, open map, bijective) directly mirrors the formal definition for a function `f`.

annotations.label: Self-homeomorphism

anchor.snippet: A self-homeomorphism is a homeomorphism from a topological space onto itself

annotations.note: Self-homeomorphisms are expressible as `X ≃ₜ X`, but Mathlib has no dedicated `SelfHomeomorph` abbreviation.

annotations.label: Homeomorphism classes

anchor.snippet: Its equivalence classes are called homeomorphism classes

annotations.note: No Mathlib decl introduces the equivalence relation 'X is homeomorphic to Y' or its quotient by name.

annotations.label: Necessity of continuous inverse

anchor.snippet: The third requirement, that

annotations.note: The article's informal observation about needing continuity of the inverse is not stated as a Mathlib lemma (e.g., no canonical counterexample on `[0,1) → S¹`).

annotations.label: Homeomorphism group

anchor.snippet: forms a group , called the homeomorphism group of X

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Homeomorph.refl`, `Homeomorph.trans`, and `Homeomorph.symm`, but no `Group (X ≃ₜ X)` instance giving the homeomorphism group structure explicitly.

annotations.label: Open interval homeomorphic to reals

anchor.snippet: is homeomorphic to the real numbers

annotations.note: `Real.tanPartialHomeomorph` realizes `tan : (-π/2, π/2) → ℝ` as an `OpenPartialHomeomorph`, but there is no derived `Homeomorph (Ioo (-π/2) (π/2)) ℝ`.

annotations.label: Closed disk homeomorphic to square

anchor.snippet: and the square

annotations.note: No homeomorphism between the closed unit disk and unit square appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graph of differentiable function

anchor.snippet: The graph of a differentiable function is homeomorphic to the domain of the function

annotations.note: Grepping for graph/homeomorph yields no statement that the graph of a (differentiable) function is homeomorphic to its domain.

annotations.label: Differentiable parametrization of a curve

anchor.snippet: A differentiable parametrization of a curve is a homeomorphism between the domain of the parametrization and the curve

annotations.note: No Mathlib lemma asserts that a differentiable parametrization of a curve is a homeomorphism onto its image.

annotations.label: Chart of a manifold

anchor.snippet: A chart of a manifold is a homeomorphism between an open subset of the manifold and an open subset of a Euclidean space

annotations.note: Charts in Mathlib are `OpenPartialHomeomorph M H` (not `Homeomorph` on open subsets), giving the same content via `chartAt H x`.

annotations.label: Stereographic projection

anchor.snippet: The stereographic projection is a homeomorphism between the unit sphere

annotations.note: `stereographic hv` is defined as an `OpenPartialHomeomorph` from the sphere minus `v` to the orthogonal complement of `v`, rather than as a packaged `Homeomorph`.

annotations.label: Topological group operations

anchor.snippet: is a topological group , its inversion map

annotations.note: `Homeomorph.mulLeft`, `Homeomorph.mulRight`, and `Homeomorph.inv` package the topological-group translations and inversion as homeomorphisms.

annotations.label: R^m not homeomorphic to R^n

anchor.snippet: are not homeomorphic for m ≠ n

annotations.note: Invariance of domain / dimension is not stated in Mathlib (no `IsEmpty (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin m) ≃ₜ EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n))` lemma).

annotations.label: Real line vs unit circle

anchor.snippet: The Euclidean real line is not homeomorphic to the unit circle

annotations.note: No Mathlib lemma states that ℝ and the unit circle (or `Circle`) are not homeomorphic.

annotations.label: Intervals not homeomorphic

anchor.snippet: The one-dimensional intervals

annotations.note: There is no Mathlib statement distinguishing open, half-open, and closed intervals up to homeomorphism.

annotations.label: Homeomorphic spaces share topological properties

anchor.snippet: Two homeomorphic spaces share the same topological properties

annotations.note: `Mathlib.Topology.Homeomorph.Lemmas` collects many transfer lemmas across homeomorphisms, but the meta-statement 'all topological properties transfer' is not itself a theorem.

annotations.label: Homeomorphism is open and closed

anchor.snippet: A homeomorphism is simultaneously an open mapping and a closed mapping

annotations.note: `Homeomorph.isOpenMap` and `Homeomorph.isClosedMap` (same file) state that every `X ≃ₜ Y` is both open and closed.

annotations.label: Alexander's trick

anchor.snippet: can be extended to a self-homeomorphism of the whole disk

annotations.note: Grep for 'Alexander' in `Mathlib/` returns no matches; Alexander's trick is not formalized.
